verificationLabel: Round 7 early setup checked Monday 13 Apr 2026
summary: Round 7 is still in preview mode as of Monday 13 April 2026, with the board carrying fresh form reads and casualty-ward checks but holding the charity bet until official team lists arrive.

sources.label: Sunday Session: Eels v Titans, Wests Tigers v Knights

scoreboard.note: Parramatta are injury-hit and coming off a 52-10 collapse, while the Bulldogs have earned real respect.
Penrith took a hit in Round 6 but still look more trustworthy than a Dolphins side missing depth.
Strong matchup, but the Cowboys carry fresh availability checks and Manly looked steady enough to trust early.
Newcastle still have key bodies in doubt and the Roosters are a steadier side right now.
The Warriors deserve the upgrade after flattening Melbourne, even with a few casualty-ward flags to monitor.
The Tigers have the momentum while the Broncos are still dealing with a brutal injury list.
Souths still need Jye Gray clarity, but the Dragons are carrying too much damage and too many losses.
Canberra come in with energy, while Melbourne now need to prove they are still a trust side.

liveAlerts.text: Official Round 7 team lists were not yet published when this board was updated on Monday, 13 April 2026.
The running charity profit stays at $11.50, so there is no pressure to force an early play.
Fresh casualty-ward concerns include Jye Gray, Samuela Fainu, Tyson Frizell, Cory Paix, Reed Mahoney and Murray Taulagi.
Tigers and Warriors earned the biggest upgrades coming out of Round 6.
Storm, Eels and Dragons all sit in the prove-it bucket heading into Round 7.
The charity play should stay open until Tuesday 14 April 2026 team lists and the first proper market reaction both land.
